post.description:
Generates a new adjustment entry attributed to a specific worker
 **Token scopes**: `adjustments:write`

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description:
## Authentication
The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

```curl
curl -X GET 'https://api.letsdeel.com/rest/v2/contracts' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

[Learn more about authentication](/api/authentication)

400.description: Bad Request – failed to create adjustments

requestBody.description: Creation data for a new adjustment

contract_id.description: The identifier of the contract associated with the adjustment

move_next_cycle.description: If an adjustments can belong to another payroll cycle.

date_of_adjustment.description: Short date in format ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). For example: 2022-12-31.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Deel adjustment id.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description:
## Authentication
The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

```curl
curl -X GET 'https://api.letsdeel.com/rest/v2/contracts' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

[Learn more about authentication](/api/authentication)

get.description:
Returns a list of payslips for a Global Payroll (GP) employee, including each payslip's date range and current status. Use this endpoint to display payslip history or verify that payslips have been published before initiating a download. Requires the worker id (UUID), which can be obtained from the List GPworkers endpoint. Each payslip in the response includes an id that is required for the download endpoint. Supports only GP contract types.
 **Token scopes**: `payslips:read`

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the worker.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description:

The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

post.description:
Synchronize time off requests from an external HRIS to Deel. Only Global Payroll contracts are allowed for this operation. This endpoint can perform 2 different operations with the provided time off list: i) upsert - time off requests identified by an external ID will be either inserted or updated at Deel. ii) delete - existing time off requests identified by an external ID with the parameter 'deleted: true' will be deleted. Deel will identify the impacts of each operation on each payroll cycle.
 **Token scopes**: `time-off:write`

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description:
## Authentication
The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

[Learn more about authentication](/api/authentication)

get.summary: Retrieve a Paginated List of Shift Rates
get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of shift rates, including details such as rate name, type, value, and timestamps. Pagination parameters can be used to control the size and position of the result set.
 **Token scopes**: `time-tracking:read`

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of records to return per page. For example, '10' to return up to 10 records.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The starting index for the records to retrieve. For example, '0' for the first page or '10' for the second page when limit=10.

post.description:
Receives timesheet file metadata and generates a link to upload timesheet file. Please note that this endpoint supports EOR contracts as of now
 **Token scopes**: `time-tracking:write`

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description:
## Authentication
The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

```curl
curl -X GET 'https://api.letsdeel.com/rest/v2/contracts' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

[Learn more about authentication](/api/authentication)

201.description: Timesheet created successfully. Returns upload URL, upload form fields, and timesheet ID.

400.description: Bad request. Validation or business rule violation (e.g., non-EOR contract, invalid date range).

422.description: Unprocessable entity. Business logic constraint violation.

requestBody.description: timesheet information needed for uploading timesheet file

get.description:
Retrieves a timesheet by ID, including its submission, review, and processing status. When expand=file_data is provided, the response also includes file-related details such as the file name, download URL.
 **Token scopes**: `time-tracking:read`

parameters.name: timesheet_id
parameters.description: ID of the timesheet to fetch file for

parameters.name: extend
parameters.description: Optional list of fields to extend in the response. Currently only `file_data` is supported, which includes file details (file_id, file_name) and download URL.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description:
## Authentication
The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

```curl
curl -X GET 'https://api.letsdeel.com/rest/v2/contracts' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

[Learn more about authentication](/api/authentication)

200.description: Timesheet retrieved successfully. Returns timesheet details including validation errors if applicable. When extend=file_data, also includes file_data object with file details and download URL.

post.description:
Review a successfully submitted timesheet. Only PENDING_REVIEW timesheets can be reviewed, and all associated hours will be approved/rejected at once.
 **Token scopes**: `time-tracking:write`

parameters.name: timesheet_id
parameters.description: ID of the timesheet to review

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description:
## Authentication
The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

```curl
curl -X GET 'https://api.letsdeel.com/rest/v2/contracts' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

[Learn more about authentication](/api/authentication)

201.description: Timesheet reviewed successfully. Returns updated timesheet details with review status and review timestamp.

422.description: Unprocessable entity. Timesheet cannot be reviewed in its current state.

requestBody.description: Review information for the timesheet. review_reason is required when review_status is REJECTED.

post.description:

get.description:

 **Token scopes**: `time-tracking:read`

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Offset/index of record for the next page of records to return.

parameters.description: Filter shifts from this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: to_date
parameters.description: Filter shifts until this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: contract_id[]
parameters.description: Filter shifts by one or more contract IDs.

amount.description: The amount in time units for this time off. When no amount is informed, it will be calculated using work schedules and public holidays defined at Deel.

end_date.description: The end date of the time off.

start_date.description: The start date of the time off.

approved_at.description: The approval date of the time off. If no approval date is informed, the start date of this request will be used as the approval date. Note: use this field in order to support retrospective leaves so Deel can identify requests made after the payroll cycle.

description.description: The description of the time off.

contract_oid.description: The contract id that has requested the time off.

requested_at.description: The request date of the time off.

entitlement_unit.description: The time unit for this time off. *Mandatory when amount parameter is informed*. If no amount and no entitlement_unit are informed, it will be calculated using Deel's internal country templates for compliant time off policies.

deleted.description: Indicates this time off request must be deleted

deelToken.description:
## Authentication
The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

```curl
curl -X GET 'https://api.letsdeel.com/rest/v2/contracts' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

[Learn more about authentication](/api/authentication)

oauth2.description: Standard OAuth2 security scheme based on https://swagger.io/docs/specification/authentication/
